plan: Cut SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and broad index exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and stability.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce semiconductor concentration.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
Trim QQQ to 15% to manage broad index exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation and a target of ~1.5%/month with significantly reduced drawdown risk. It prioritizes stability by cutting leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Reduce QQQI to 15% to balance income with growth.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Trim SMH to 10% and CRWD to 10% to reduce concentration.
Buy XLK at 5% for quality tech exposure.
Maintain a ~10% cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month with a focus on risk-first growth, blending broad index with quality tech. It reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for moderate drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Reduce QQQI to 10% to free up capital for growth.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase SMH to 15% and CRWD to 15% for aggressive growth.
Buy META at 10% as a top scanner setup.
Maintain a ~5% cash buffer.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leaning into high-scoring setups and using leverage. Be aware this strategy significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential, and leveraged ETFs can decay daily.

moves.note: Reduce broad index for income focus

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize income allocation.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Sell SMH and CRWD to exit high-beta single names.
Trim QQQ to 10% to focus on income.
Hold a cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month by rotating into income ETFs and broad index for steady monthly distributions. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
